summary:
The available literature lacks specific data comparing bleeding risks between bare-metal stents and alternative treatments in high-bleeding-risk patients during PCI. Due to insufficient evidence on hemorrhage risk or optimal antithrombotic management for this subgroup, it remains uncertain whether bare-metal stenting is justifiable in these individuals.  

Note: This summary adheres strictly to only clinically pertinent facts from Agent2’s analysis—excluding non-clinical elements—and maintains focus solely on patient-specific outcomes related to procedure justification under defined risk conditions.
